title: Lake Blanche
subtitle: Big Cottonwood Canyon, Utah — Twin Peaks Wilderness
summary:
## The Plan

This is the big one for the season. Two nights at the basin, fishing the lake, scrambling Sundial if the weather holds. Boys have been training on shorter overnighters all summer and they're ready.

## Route

In from the Mill B South trailhead. The lower switchbacks are the worst of it — relentless granite stairs for the first couple miles. Then it eases through the bench before the final climb to the basin.

> Total: ~3.4 miles each way, 2,700 ft of climbing. Pace ourselves.

## Bear Game Plan

This is black bear country and we've had reports near the lake the last two seasons. Hard rules:

- All food, trash, and smelly items in the BV500 every night
- Cook downwind, 200 ft from tent
- Rinse hands and dishes well away from camp
- Bear spray on Dad's hip belt during the day

## What I'm Watching

Weather is the wildcard. September in the Wasatch can flip from t-shirt to snow in 12 hours. Pulling forecasts the night before and willing to bail to a one-nighter if a system rolls in.

*— Trip log to be updated post-trip.*
